commit.message:
Fix truncated Gemini responses: disable thinking mode, increase token limit

Gemini 2.5 Flash uses thinking mode by default which consumes output
tokens internally, leaving very few for the actual response. Disabled
thinking (thinkingBudget: 0) since prompt expansion doesn't need
reasoning, and increased maxOutputTokens from 500 to 2048.

commit.message:
Fix Render health check timeout: defer heavy imports to request time

Removed spaCy model loading from FastAPI lifespan startup — it was
blocking the app from accepting requests and causing the 5 s health
check to time out on Render free tier.  Heavy service imports
(litellm, pandas, fairlearn, presidio, spacy) are now deferred to
inside the handler functions that use them, so /health responds
instantly at startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix shared example breaking on Ruby 3.1

On Ruby 3.1, a proc with |objects, type: :x| called with a single
array argument destructures it — so objects receives the first
element instead of the full array. Ruby 3.2+ changed this behavior,
which is why the test was passing in CI (Ruby 3.3) but failed once
we pinned the local version to the minimum required Ruby (3.1.0).

Using a splat (*objects) avoids the destructuring entirely: it
collects all positional arguments into an array, so objects.first
is always the classes array regardless of Ruby version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Log raw LLM responses in tournament draft before asserts (#951)

## Summary

- When `_llm_pick` gets an empty response from OpenRouter (e.g. the Opus
4.6 empty-content crash), the assertion fires before anything is written
to the draft JSONL, leaving zero evidence of what the API returned.
- Add a `_log_llm_call` helper that writes the full raw response
(`response.model_dump()`) plus prompts and pick metadata to the draft
log **before** any asserts run, for both the initial call and retry.
- The existing post-success log entry is kept (now typed
`"pick_result"`) for the parsed pick/cost data, but no longer duplicates
prompts already captured in the `llm_call` entry.

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` passes (lint, typecheck, tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sky view height: use explicit JS sizing instead of flex

Root cause: flex:1 on .sky-view depends on parent having resolved
height. On mobile browsers 100vh/100dvh can be unreliable during
layout, giving the flex child 0 height. The canvas then draws into
a 0-height area = blank screen.

Fix: sizeSkyView() calculates available height from window.innerHeight
minus the fixed elements (top bar, compass, focus card) and sets an
explicit pixel height on .sky-view. Called on app start and resize.

commit.message:
feat: organic murmuration with wandering attractors

- 4 invisible attractor points drift on slow sine paths
- Each bird assigned a primary attractor, creating sub-flocks
- Sub-flocks stretch into streams/funnels as attractors move apart
- Strong alignment + weak cohesion = ribbons not blobs
- Per-bird wander noise prevents lockstep movement

commit.message:
Classify InterfaceClassWithParamOk → §8.26.1 [skip ci]

The test declares a bare interface class containing only a pure virtual function method. §8.26.1 defines the interface class syntax including the rule interface_class_method ::= pure virtual method_prototype ; — the exact construct exercised. §8.26.3 ("Type access") covers parameter/typedef inheritance between interface classes, which this test does not exercise.

commit.message:
fix: delegate reprocess to GPU worker instead of downloading locally

reprocessManager now checks shouldDelegate() for ALL stages, not just
audio-dependent ones. Text-only stages (redacting, assembling, reviewing)
are delegated to the GPU worker with a fromStage param so the worker
downloads S3 prerequisites and runs the agent there.
